name: OPNsense
author: InSpectre Community
description: Integrates with OPNsense firewall via its built-in REST API. Discovers ARP table entries for device inventory, and blocks/unblocks devices by managing a dedicated firewall alias (inspectre_blocked) that feeds a LAN block rule.

config_schema.label: OPNsense Host
config_schema.help: Base URL of your OPNsense web interface, e.g. https://192.168.1.1. Do not include a trailing slash.

config_schema.label: API Key
config_schema.help: API key generated in System > Access > Users > your user > API keys. The key acts as the HTTP Basic auth username.

config_schema.label: API Secret
config_schema.help: API secret paired with the API key above. Acts as the HTTP Basic auth password.

config_schema.label: Block Alias Name
config_schema.help: Name of the OPNsense Host alias that InSpectre will add/remove blocked IPs from. Create this alias in Firewall > Aliases (type: Host) and add a LAN block rule sourcing it before enabling blocking.

config_schema.label: Verify SSL Certificate
config_schema.help: Disable if your OPNsense instance uses a self-signed certificate (default for most home installs).

get_arp_table.description: Fetch the ARP table from OPNsense diagnostics. Returns all known LAN hosts with IP and MAC.

get_dhcp_leases.description: Fetch active DHCPv4 leases from OPNsense for richer hostname and MAC data.

get_alias_uuid.description: Resolve the UUID for the configured block alias by name. Required before adding/removing IPs.

block_client.description: Add the device IP to the block alias using alias_util, then apply the alias to the live pf tables immediately (no full rule reload needed).

unblock_client.description: Remove the device IP from the block alias using alias_util. Takes effect immediately in the live pf table.

get_blocked_list.description: List all IPs currently in the block alias.

flush_blocked.description: Flush (clear) all IPs from the block alias. Use with caution — unblocks everything.

test_connection.description: Verify API credentials by fetching firmware version info.
